nodes.context: Japan's largest yakuza organization, led by Kazuo Taoka from 1946 to 1981.
An inter-yakuza confederation that Taoka refused to join in 1963.
A yakuza gang that formed an alliance with Yamaguchi-gumi in 1972.
A rival gang to Yamaguchi-gumi; member attempted to assassinate Taoka in 1978.

edges.evidence_quote: he notably refused to join the Kanto-kai, an inter-yakuza confederation in 1963.
edges.context: Taoka refused to join the Kanto-kai, indicating non-cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: Taoka forged an alliance between the Yamaguchi-gumi and the Inagawa-kai at a sakazuki ceremony at his home.
edges.context: Taoka formed an alliance between Yamaguchi-gumi and Inagawa-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: Taoka survived a 1978 assassination attempt when he was shot in the back of the neck by a member of the Matsuda-gumi (松田組), a rival gang to the Yamaguchi-gumi
edges.context: Matsuda-gumi attempted to assassinate Taoka, indicating conflict.
